channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Toffoli voou em aviăo de empresa de Vorcaro para ir ao Tayayá, indicam documentos da Anac
item.description: Documents from Anac and Decea indicate that STF Minister Dias Toffoli flew on July 4, 2025, on an aircraft operated by Prime Aviation, a company in which Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, was a shareholder. The flight was destined for Tayayá, raising concerns about potential conflicts of interest and the use of private resources by public officials. Folha de S.P.H. obtained the records, which suggest a connection between the minister and a financial sector businessman.

item.title: Crusoé: Lula posa de defensor do Pix
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) defended the Pix payment system after it was cited in a U.S. government report as a potential trade barrier. His remarks were prompted and completed by Communications Minister Sidônio Palmeira, who reminded Lula to explicitly mention Pix during his statement.

item.title: Governo explora críticas dos EUA ao Pix e amplia confronto entre Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The U.S. government criticized Brazil's Pix payment system in a trade report, claiming it distorts international commerce. The Lula administration used the criticism to reinforce its national sovereignty narrative, accusing Flávio Bolsonaro of seeking foreign interference in Brazilian elections through ties to Donald Trump and participation in CPAC. Lula and other officials defended Pix as a national success and refused to change the model.

item.title: PT vai usar inserção na TV para atacar Flávio Bolsonaro em estreia de nova estratégia de Lula
item.description: The PT will use its TV and radio ad time to attack Flávio Bolsonaro, marking the debut of a new strategy in Lula's campaign. The offensive follows polls showing Lula stagnating and Bolsonaro rising, including a technical tie in a potential second-round scenario according to Genial Quaest. The strategy also includes social media actions and grassroots work in key cities, linking Bolsonaro to an alleged 'political project based on attacking democracy'.

item.title: PT reage a relatório de Trump sobre Pix e ataca Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The PT reacted to U.S. government criticism of Brazil's Pix payment system, accusing Senator Flávio Bolsonaro of supporting Donald Trump to undermine the system. The party defends Pix as a symbol of national sovereignty, emphasizing it was created by Brazil and should not be changed due to foreign pressure. Lula stated that no one will change Pix, given its service to Brazilian society.

item.title: Tarcísio sanciona lei que autoriza SP a fazer empréstimos de R$ 15 bi
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) signed a law authorizing the São Paulo state government to take out loans of up to US$ 2.8 billion (about R$ 15 billion) to fund infrastructure and social projects. The funds will primarily support the completion of the Rodoanel (North, East, and West sections), the Poverty Overcoming Program, state debt sustainability improvements, and a public-private partnership for the New Administrative Center. The measure aims to fulfill existing contractual obligations, covering the gap between initial cost estimates and approved updated project costs.

item.title: Alckmin diz que dois estados decidiram não aderir à subvenção ao diesel importado
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin announced that only Rio de Janeiro and Rondônia have refused to join the federal subsidy program for imported diesel, while two or three other states are still evaluating their position until Friday. The measure, which proposes a R$1.20 per liter subsidy (R$0.60 from the Union and R$0.60 from states), aims to mitigate the impact of rising oil prices due to Middle East tensions and prevent cost pass-through to consumers, with a temporary duration of up to two months.

item.title: “Lula e nós salvamos a democracia”, diz Alckmin
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin (PSB) stated that he and President Lula (PT) saved democracy by winning the last elections. Confirmed on the reelection ticket, he said the campaign should, at the appropriate time, compare Lula's government with that of former President Jair Bolsonaro (PL).

item.title: Ministro da Previdência desiste de candidatura e segue no governo Lula
item.description: Social Security Minister Wolney Queiroz (PDT) announced he is withdrawing his candidacy for the Chamber of Deputies in 2026 at President Lula’s request, choosing to remain in office until December to focus on combating fraud and reducing INSS backlogs. He assumed the role in May 2025 after Carlos Lupi resigned amid an investigation into illegal pension deductions. Queiroz, a six-term federal deputy who ran unsuccessfully for re-election in 2022, has been a PDT member since 1992.

item.title: Congresso reprova ou deixa caducar 77% das MPs de Lula; taxa de aprovação é a menor desde 2001
item.description: Congress approved only 23% of the provisional measures issued by Lula in his third government, the lowest rate since the EC 32 of 2001, with 77% of MPs expiring without deliberation. This 'silent veto' reflects the declining effectiveness of the executive instrument over the past two decades, regardless of the party in power. The downward trend in approval rates began after the peak of 90.4% in Lula's first term and reached 68.3% under Bolsonaro.

item.title: O que explica o crescimento de Flávio Bolsonaro nas pesquisas recentes, segundo Marinho
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro's rise in the polls is attributed to increased public awareness of his political profile, shifting perception from a regional senator to a conservative, dialoguing, and flexible candidate. According to Rogério Marinho, coordinator of the pre-campaign, voters now see him as a young, negotiable figure who is not inflexible, overcoming initial rejection due to lack of familiarity.

item.title: “Não tem apoio ao Flávio? Descarte-o, ainda que do PL”, diz Eduardo
item.description: Former deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro urged supporters on Thursday (2) not to vote for candidates who do not support his brother Flávio Bolsonaro, even if they are from the PL party. He argued that voting for those who do not support Flávio only helps Lula and advised voters to apply this criterion in elections for senator, federal deputy, or state deputy.

item.title: Lula diz que Petrobras vai anular leilão que elevou preço do gás de cozinha
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced that Petrobras will annul the LPG (cooking gas) auction held on Tuesday, which resulted in prices above reference levels and was deemed an act of insubordination against government and company leadership. Lula criticized the auction as 'cretinice and bandidagem,' stating that poor Brazilians will not bear the cost of the Middle East conflict, and emphasized that the government will not allow increases in the 13kg gas cylinder, which could rise by up to R$ 39.40.

item.title: Com transmissão na TV, Universal prepara megaevento em 9 estádios
item.description: The Universal Church of the Kingdom of God is holding the 'Family at the Foot of the Cross' event across nine Brazilian stadiums and arenas this Good Friday, with TV Record broadcast and free admission. Bishop Renato Cardoso, positioned as Edir Macedo’s successor, and his wife Cristiane Cardoso serve as ambassadors, emphasizing spiritual solutions to family crises. While framed as evangelistic, the event has drawn skepticism from former members regarding its actual motives.

item.title: TSE retoma caso Denarium após adiamentos que permitiram renúncia estratégica
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) will resume on Friday, April 10, the trial that could render former Roraima governor Antonio Denarium ineligible, following a series of delays that allowed him to remain in office until his strategic resignation last Friday, April 27. He was convicted in 2022 by the Roraima Regional Electoral Court for the irregular use of social programs ('Cesta da Família' and 'Morar Melhor') to gain electoral advantages, and now faces disqualification from running for Senate in the 2026 elections, similar to what happened to Cláudio Castro in Rio de Janeiro.

item.title: Fernando Morais interrompe entrevista para atender ligação de Lula
item.description: Writer Fernando Morais interrupted a live interview on the Jornal da Bahia no Ar program to take a call from President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who wanted to know about the launch of the second volume of his biography, 'Lula, Volume 2'. Morais returned to the interview and said Lula called to ask how the event in São Paulo went, where he developed tendinitis from signing many autographs.

item.title: Virada no TSE e aposta no desconhecido: o jogo arriscado do PL de Flávio no Rio
item.description: Governor Cláudio Castro is seeking to overturn his ineligibility at the TSE through potential changes in the Court’s composition, aiming to remain viable for a future Senate run. Meanwhile, the Rio PL is backing little-known candidate Douglas Ruas for the state election — initially favoring an indirect vote via the State Legislative Assembly to avoid a direct clash with Eduardo Paes, but preparing a direct candidacy as a long-term strategy to build name recognition, with Flávio Bolsonaro acting as a key behind-the-scenes operator.

item.title: É defesa do emprego e da renda, diz Alckmin sobre taxa das blusinhas
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin defended the 'blusinhas tax' as a measure to protect employment and income in Brazil's apparel sector, stating that domestic producers still pay more taxes than importers even with the levy. He noted that the taxation on international purchases under US$50, in effect since August 2024, aims to ensure fair competition and was inserted into the Mover Law as a 'jabuti'. Alckmin said he does not participate in the debate about the impact of removing this tax on the 2026 elections.

item.title: Wolney Queiroz desiste de candidatura e seguirá na Previdência Social
item.description: Minister of Social Security Wolney Queiroz (PDT) announced he will not leave his position to run in the 2026 elections, following guidance from President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT). He will remain in office to continue key government actions, particularly managing the INSS, reducing backlogs, and overseeing reimbursement programs. The decision aims to preserve stability in sensitive areas by avoiding the departure of technical officials during the electoral process, reflecting Planalto’s concern over criticisms from the INSS CPMI.

item.title: Coordenador de campanha descarta novo ‘Posto Ipiranga’ para Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Rogério Marinho, coordinator of Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-campaign, dismissed the need for a new 'Ipiranga Post' in the economy, stating that fiscal responsibility was already established during Bolsonaro's government and there is no longer uncertainty about the right's economic management. He criticized the Lula government for fiscal irresponsibility, tax increases, and high interest rates, and said Flávio Bolsonaro's proposal aims to revive deregulation, legal security, and private sector partnerships.

item.title: Eliziane deixa o PSD após escolha por Caiado e se filia ao PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama (PT-MA) left the PSD and joined the PT on April 2, 2026, after the party selected Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate. She criticized the PSD’s new political direction, stating her thinking differs, and reaffirmed her commitment to social justice in Maranhão. Eliziane was the rapporteur of the January 8 CPMI that recommended the indictment of Jair Bolsonaro and 60 others.

item.title: Aliados veem pavimentação de caminho para Nunes disputar governo de SP em 2030
item.description: Allies of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas and São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes believe the path for the emedebista to run for governor of São Paulo in 2030 is being paved, especially after Vice Governor Felicio Ramuth joined the MDB. This move is seen as part of a 'married game' to strengthen Nunes’ ticket, with explicit support from Tarcísio, who had previously signaled preference for Nunes as his successor. Ramuth’s exit from the PSD, following disagreements with Gilberto Kassab over the vice-governor slot, underscores the strategic alignment between parties for the state succession.

item.title: Lula defende o Pix após críticas em relatório comercial dos EUA
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva defended Brazil’s Pix payment system after criticism in a U.S. Trade Representative report, stating that Pix belongs to Brazil and will not be changed due to external pressure. He emphasized that Pix should be improved to meet Brazilians’ needs and highlighted its importance to the national financial system. The U.S. report claims the Central Bank of Brazil gives preferential treatment to Pix, disadvantaging foreign competitors like WhatsApp Pay.

item.title: AtlasIntel indica Moro eleito governador do PR no primeiro turno
item.description: AtlasIntel poll released on Thursday shows Senator Sergio Moro (PL-PR) with over 50% voting intention for governor of Paraná, securing a first-round victory. The former Operation Car Wash judge left União Brasil to join the PL in order to make his candidacy viable.

item.title: Lula tem a menor taxa de aprovação de propostas no Congresso desde a redemocratização
item.description: Lula has the lowest approval rate of executive proposals in Congress since redemocratization, with only 28% of approved federal legislation in 2025 originating exclusively from the Presidency, according to the Ranking dos Políticos. This reflects a more autonomous and ideologically opposed Congress, with greater legislative initiative from lawmakers and lower conversion of executive bills into law.

item.title: Rui Costa encerra 3 anos na Casa Civil e passa bastão a Miriam Belchior
item.description: Rui Costa ended his 3-year and 3-month tenure as Chief of Staff of the Casa Civil in a ceremony alongside President Lula in Salvador, with Miriam Belchior succeeding him. The event featured the delivery of Novo PAC urban mobility and slope containment projects, underscoring Costa’s central role in political, fiscal, and budgetary coordination, including IOF debates and the push for public disclosure of social achievements such as reduced hunger and unemployment.

item.title: Haddad avalia pesquisa Atlas/Estadão para o governo de SP: ‘Começar por onde terminei é bom começo’
item.description: Fernando Haddad, the PT’s pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo, welcomed the AtlasIntel/Estadão poll showing him at 42.6% in the first round, behind Tarcísio de Freitas at 49.1%. He said 'starting where I left off is a good start,' referencing his 2022 runoff loss with 44.73%. Haddad noted his strength in the São Paulo metropolitan area in 2022, criticized Tarcísio for lacking ties to the interior, and accused the governor of being mentally anchored in Brasília.

item.title: Trump pode ajudar Lula de novo se insistir em pressionar por mudança no PIX
item.description: Lula reaffirmed his support for Brazil’s PIX instant payment system during a speech in Bahia, rejecting external pressures to change it following a new U.S. government report criticizing the tool for harming American credit card companies. The defense of PIX, previously used by Lula to gain political traction amid U.S. trade pressures, may again boost his popularity amid rising disapproval, especially as it is framed as a nationalist issue against Bolsonaro-linked opposition. Petista activists are now linking the criticism of PIX to PL presidential candidate Flavio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Alckmin defende ajuste fiscal sobre “privilégios”, e não sobre pobres
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin argued that fiscal adjustment should target privileges and waste, not the poor, aligning with President Lula. He said the government aims to do more with fewer resources in an ongoing effort. However, economists and financial institutions criticize the fiscal policy, as gross debt reached 79.2% of GDP in February, equivalent to R$10.2 trillion.

item.title: Alckmin defende comparação entre Lula e Bolsonaro como estratégia de campanha: 'Quem defende ditaduranão deveria ser candidato'
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin defended that the Lula re-election campaign should invest in comparing the Lula and Bolsonaro governments as a political strategy, stating the action will occur at the 'adequate moment'. He said 'President Lula and we saved democracy' and that 'who defends dictatorship should not even be a candidate', while downplaying the rise of Flávio Bolsonaro in polls.

item.title: Lula exonera Camilo Santana do MEC e nomeia novo ministro
item.description: President Lula dismissed Senator Camilo Santana from the Ministry of Education to bolster his re-election campaign in Ceará, appointing Leonardo Barchini, the former executive secretary, as the new minister. Barchini, a senior CAPES official and close ally of Haddad and Padilha, was announced during the simultaneous inauguration of 107 education projects nationwide, highlighting 99,000 schools with adequate connectivity and R$ 413.49 million in investments from the New PAC for school construction, federal institute campuses, and university hospital reforms.

item.title: Por que o relatório dos EUA contra Moraes pode afetar a disputa Lula x Flávio
item.description: A U.S. House Judiciary Committee report criticizes Brazilian Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes for alleged restrictions on freedom of speech, which opposition figures like Flávio Bolsonaro are using to attack the Lula government. The narrative ties judicial criticism to Lula’s image, as many voters associate Moraes with the administration, potentially influencing voter perception in the 2026 presidential race.

item.title: Quem defende a ditadura não deveria ser candidato, diz Alckmin
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin (PSB) stated that those who defend dictatorship should not be candidates, indirectly criticizing pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) and framing the election as 'democracy versus dictatorship'. He said the Lula government saved democracy, expressed honor at being invited to join the re-election ticket, and described running as an 'act of love'. Alckmin also advocated reducing Brazil's high number of political parties, currently at 30.

item.title: “Nunca nem tratei sobre Cleitinho em PL ou não”, diz Nikolas
item.description: Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) denied blocking Senator Cleitinho’s (Republicanos-MG) entry into the PL party, stating the matter was never brought to him for a decision. The denial came amid speculation that Cleitinho could become the PL’s candidate for governor of Minas Gerais with the support of Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president. Ferreira emphasized he lacks the authority to veto Cleitinho’s party affiliation.

item.title: "Pix é do Brasil e ninguém vai fazer a gente mudar", diz Lula ao citar relatório dos EUA
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated on Thursday (2) that Brazil's Pix instant payment system, launched in 2020 as a free service, will not be changed despite a U.S. report on the matter. He emphasized that 'Pix is Brazil's and nobody will make us change it'.

item.title: Sob críticas de Lula, Petrobras estuda cancelar leilão de gás de cozinha que teve ágio de 117%
item.description: Petrobras is studying the cancellation of a cooking gas auction after criticism from President Lula, as the bidding ended with premiums above 100%, raising concerns about the impact on final consumer prices.

item.title: Lula rebate Trump, diz que Pix é do Brasil e não será alterado
item.description: President Lula stated that Pix is a Brazilian creation and will not be changed due to external pressure, following U.S. criticism in the USTR report. He emphasized that the government can improve Pix to better serve the population but will not yield to international demands labeling it as trade-distorting. The remarks were made during a visit to Salvador’s VLT construction site, after a direct request from Secom Minister Sidônio Palmeira.

item.title: Como as mudanças no chocolate podem impactar os futuros ovos de Páscoa
item.description: A bill approved by the Chamber of Deputies seeks to regulate the minimum cocoa content in chocolates in Brazil, aiming to counteract ultraprocessing practices that reduce cocoa and increase sugar and fat, making products like Easter eggs overly sweet and less authentic. The proposal sets specific cocoa percentages for each chocolate type (dark, milk, white, etc.) and requires these figures to be visible on over 15% of the front packaging. The text now moves to the Senate for debate, where, if passed, it will be sent to President Lula for sanction or veto.

item.title: Conflitos por preços de diesel e gás pressionam governo
item.description: President Lula announced he will annul Petrobras' LPG auction, calling it 'cretinice' and 'bandidagem' due to over 100% agio, stating the poor will not pay for the war's costs. Meanwhile, fuel distributors Vibra, Ipiranga, and Raízen refuse to join the diesel subsidy program, maintaining high profits despite pressure to lower fuel taxes. Eduardo Bolsonaro threatens to report alleged TSE bias to the Trump administration, while Minister Toffoli faces criticism for using private jets for official travel.

item.title: Senadora alega insatisfação com candidatura presidencial de Caiado, deixa PSD e se filia ao PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama (MA) left the PSD and joined the PT following the confirmation of Ronaldo Caiado’s presidential pre-candidacy by the same party, stating the PSD had taken a 'new track' with a more right-wing figure. She aims to help Lula win re-election in October and had been part of the government base in the Senate, serving as a bridge to the evangelical community. Her departure reduces the PSD’s Senate bench from 13 to 11 senators, following Rodrigo Pacheco’s move to the PSB.

item.title: Lula quer mandar bancos darem desconto em dívidas, diz Durigan
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan announced the government is studying guarantees to enable banks and fintechs to apply broad discounts on debt renegotiations, reducing the outstanding balance before refinancing, with potential financial education requirements. The initiative, a priority of President Lula, aims to simplify access relative to the Desenrola Brasil program, functioning as a risk-reducing guarantee rather than a subsidy.

item.title: Gonet defende livrar Moro de inclusão no Inquérito das Milícias Digitais
item.description: Attorney General Paulo Gonet recommended that the STF reject the request to include Senator Sergio Moro in the Digital Militias Inquiry, stating that the petitioner, federal deputy Lindbergh Farias, lacks active legitimacy to bring the case before the court. The final decision rests with Minister Alexandre de Moraes, who has not yet ruled. Moro, a pre-candidate for governor of Paraná, had made unsubstantiated claims that the 2022 election was illegitimate, prompting the inquiry request.

item.title: Lula reforça soberania sobre o Pix nas redes após críticas dos EUA
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva reaffirmed Brazil's sovereignty over the Pix payment system following U.S. criticism that it distorts international trade. He posted on social media: 'Pix is Brazil's' and declared at an event in Salvador that 'no one will make Brazil change the Pix'.

item.title: A conversa de Lula e Pacheco antes de filiação do senador ao PSB
item.description: One hour before joining the PSB, Senator Rodrigo Pacheco spoke with President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who wished him luck in his new party. The expectation within the party and from Lula is that Pacheco will run for governor of Minas Gerais and provide a political platform for Lula’s re-election bid in the state. Although he did not confirm his candidacy, Pacheco indicated that talks to form a progressive slate in Minas will begin next week.

item.title: Combo que liga STF a Vorcaro pressiona PGR a investigar ministros
item.description: Alleged connections between Supreme Court ministers and former banker Daniel Vorcaro are increasing pressure on the PGR to investigate potential irregularities tied to Banco Master. The revealed links involve suspicions of favoritism and conflicts of interest, sparking debates over judicial independence. The Prosecutor General's Office faces demands to deepen its inquiries within ongoing investigations.

item.title: Alckmin diz que 'quem defende ditadura não devia nem ser candidato'
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin stated that those who defend dictatorship should not even be candidates, referring to PL's pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro. He announced his departure from the MDIC to run on Lula's ticket in 2026, arguing that elections will be decided once campaigning begins and criticizing Brazil's excessive multiparty system.

item.title: "Pix é do Brasil e ninguém vai fazer a gente mudar", diz Lula após críticas da Casa Branca
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva defended Brazil's Pix instant payment system as a national asset, rejecting White House claims that it creates unfair competition for U.S. firms like Visa and Mastercard. Lula stated that 'nobody will make us change Pix', highlighting its role in financial inclusion and serving the needs of the Brazilian population. The remarks were made during a Novo PAC event in Salvador, following a prompt from Communications Minister Sidônio Palmeira, and were later amplified on social media with the slogan 'Pix é do Brasil'.

item.title: Lula rebate críticas do governo Trump: ‘Ninguém nos fará mudar o Pix’
item.description: President Lula defended Brazil's Pix instant payment system during a visit to urban mobility works in Salvador, stating that no one will make Brazil change Pix despite criticisms from the Trump administration. He also labeled opposition groups as 'fascists' and vowed to prevent their return to power, linking adversaries like Flávio Bolsonaro to Trump's decisions as part of the PT's electoral strategy.

item.title: Como Lula trabalha para adiantar a sabatina de Messias no Senado
item.description: President Lula is working to speed up the confirmation hearing of Jorge Messias in the Senate, aiming for his approval to the Supreme Federal Court before the 2026 elections. Although he has not met personally with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, Lula seeks to ensure the nominee already has the minimum support needed for approval in the Constitution and Justice Committee and later in the plenary. Messias will be on vacation for the next three weeks but will remain in Brasília to gather votes.

item.title: Novo vídeo expõe armas da campanha de Flávio Bolsonaro contra Lula
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro released a 53-second pre-campaign video focused on the economy, criticizing rising fuel and electricity costs, and proposing a leaner state with lower taxes to benefit workers and businesses. Although he did not mention Lula directly, the video indirectly criticized the current government by rejecting 'populist promises' and advocating for 'results'. The campaign avoided traditional bolsonarist themes such as civic-military schools or attacks on electronic voting machines, concentrating solely on the country's economic situation.
